content:
I keep getting the same call from AI founders asking for clients. The pattern in what they’ve built explains why most of them won’t make it.

6 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by Immo Wegmann on UnsplashA few weeks ago, an AI startup founder messaged me asking for help getting clients. Nothing unusual about that, I get a handful of these every month now. Voice AI agents. Chatbots. AI lead generation tools. AI sales tools. Different logos, different decks, same underlying ask: we built the thing, now we need customers.
What strikes me every time isn’t the product. It’s how similar they all are. I could take five decks from five different founders this year, black out the branding, and most people wouldn’t be able to tell you which one belongs to which startup. Same voice agent pitch. Same chatbot pitch. Same “AI powered sales assistant that books meetings for you” pitch. The technology works. Nobody disputes that. The problem is that everyone built the same thing at roughly the same time, and now they’re all fighting over the same small pool of buyers who have already been pitched this exact product by three other founders that week.
One of them, a founder building a voice AI agent, put it to me directly. He said something like, everyone is building the same product, voice…
